text: The surgeon performs laparoscopic adhesiolysis using cold scissors to create space for robotic trocars, carefully dissecting around the previous surgical site and visualizing the staple line from the prior sleeve gastrectomy.

The surgeon opens the lesser omentum and fully dissects the stomach at the hiatus. A panel strain is used to encircle the stomach, facilitating retraction and improving exposure of the herniated stomach for further dissection.
The surgeon carefully identifies and removes previous non-absorbable stitches, extending the dissection into the mediastinum to expose the intrathoracic esophagus.

The surgeon places non-observable interrupted stitches posterior to the esophagus, approximating the crura. A FASIX mesh is then positioned encircling the hiatus and covering the repair, and it is secured to the diaphragm with additional interrupted stitches.
